Issue: Memory constraints when training diffusion models on large images (12 channels, 256x256) causing out-of-memory (OOM) errors even with batch size 1
Decision: Reduce model size of Diffusion_AE to accommodate larger data
Rationale: Reducing model size can help mitigate memory issues during training, though the exact method is unclear. Alternative approaches like random cropping or using flash attention are suggested to reduce memory usage.
Author: karllandheer
Description: Diffusion_AE model and training pipeline for 2D diffusion autoencoder
Cost: High memory usage due to large image size (256x256, 12 channels) and model complexity
Risk: Out-of-memory (OOM) errors when training on large images, potential trade-offs in model performance when reducing size or using cropping transforms

Argument.author: karllandheer
Argument.argument: Reducing model size of Diffusion_AE is a potential solution to address memory constraints when training on larger images.

Argument.author: virginiafdez
Argument.argument: Using flash attention, removing attention layers, or reducing the number of channels/residual blocks per level can make attention layers more efficient and reduce memory usage.

Argument.author: virginiafdez
Argument.argument: Applying center-crop transforms to reduce input size is another way to mitigate memory issues, though it may complicate inference.

Justification: The issue explicitly mentions model size reduction and attention layer adjustments ('channels', 'layers'), which are direct indicators of Modeling Development. The focus is on training-related memory constraints ('OOM errors', 'batch size 1'), confirming the Modeling Development stage.
